title: agrostis stolonifera var. densiflora
intro: Agrostis stolonifera var. densiflora, also known as Creeping Bentgrass, is a perennial grass species native to Asia and Europe, characterized by its creeping stolons and dense inflorescence. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to help alleviate various health issues such as digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Creeping Bentgrass include its ability to improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Creeping Bentgrass is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in topical applications for wound care and skin health.
